questions.q: Why must swap be disabled on all Kubernetes nodes before running kubeadm?
options: Swap causes iptables rules to be reset on node reboot
kubelet requires predictable memory behaviour and will fail to start if swap is active
Swap prevents etcd from persisting data correctly to disk
Swap is incompatible with CNI plugins like Calico
questions.explanation: kubelet (by default) refuses to run if swap is enabled because Kubernetes memory management and OOM handling rely on predictable memory limits. With swap active, a container's memory usage becomes unpredictable and resource limits cannot be reliably enforced.

questions.q: After running `kubeadm init` successfully, what is the correct way to configure kubectl access for the cluster administrator?
options: Run kubectl config init to auto-detect the cluster
Copy /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf to ~/.kube/config and set correct ownership
Set the KUBECONFIG_PATH environment variable to /etc/kubernetes
questions.explanation: kubeadm init prints the exact commands: mkdir -p $HOME/.kube && cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config && chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config. This copies the admin kubeconfig (with embedded cluster CA and client cert) to the standard kubectl location.

questions.q: Which kernel modules must be loaded on all nodes before kubeadm can initialise a cluster?
questions.explanation: The overlay module is required by containerd for OverlayFS storage (layered container images). The br_netfilter module enables bridge network traffic to pass through iptables, which is required for kube-proxy and pod networking (controlled via the net.bridge.bridge-nf-call-iptables sysctl).
